name: smp

metadata.name: smp
classifiers: License :: OSI Approved :: Apache Software License
metadata.summary: Simple Management Protocol (SMP) for remotely managing MCU firmware

documentation:
# Simple Management Protocol (SMP)

`smp` implements serialization and deserialization of the Simple Management Protocol.

The SMP specification can be found [here](https://docs.zephyrproject.org/latest/services/device_mgmt/smp_protocol.html).

If you'd like a library that implements the **serial (UART or USB)**, **Bluetooth (BLE)**, and
**UDP** transport layers for SMP, take a look at
[smpclient](https://github.com/intercreate/smpclient).

If you need an SMP CLI application to interact with device firmware, then try
[smpmgr](https://github.com/intercreate/smpmgr).

## Install

`smp` is [distributed by PyPI](https://pypi.org/project/smp/) and can be installed with `poetry`, `pip`, and other dependency managers.

## User Documentation

Documentation is in the source code so that it is available to your editor.
An online version is generated and available [here](https://jphutchins.github.io/smp/).

## Development Quickstart

> Assumes that you've already [setup your development environment](#development-environment-setup).

1. activate [envr](https://github.com/JPhutchins/envr), the environment manager for **bash**, **zsh**, and **PS**:
   ```
   . ./envr.ps1
   ```
2. run `poetry install` when pulling in new changes
3. run `lint` after making changes
4. run `test` after making changes
5. add library dependencies with `poetry`:
   ```
   poetry add <my_new_dependency>
   ```
6. add test or other development dependencies using [poetry groups](https://python-poetry.org/docs/managing-dependencies#dependency-groups):
   ```
   poetry add -G dev <my_dev_dependency>
   ```
7.  run tests for all supported python versions:
   ```
   tox
   ```

## Development Environment Setup

### Install Dependencies

- poetry: https://python-poetry.org/docs/#installation

### Create the venv

```
poetry install
```

The `venv` should be installed to `.venv`.

### Activate envr

> [envr](https://github.com/JPhutchins/envr) supports **bash**, **zsh**, and **PS** in Linux, MacOS, and Windows.  If you are using an unsupported shell, you can activate the `.venv` environment manually, use `poetry run` and `poetry shell`, and refer to `envr-default` for useful aliases.

```
. ./envr.ps1
```

### Verify Your Setup

To verify the installation, make sure that all of the tests are passing using these envr aliases:

```
lint
test
```

### Enable the githooks

> The pre-commit hook will run the linters but not the unit tests.

```
git config core.hooksPath .githooks
```

modules.name: smp.file_management
documentation: The Simple Management Protocol (SMP) File Management group.

classes.name: smp.file_management.FileDownloadRequest

class_variables.name: smp.file_management.FileDownloadRequest.off
documentation: Offset in the file to read from.

class_variables.name: smp.file_management.FileDownloadRequest.name
documentation: Name of the file to download.

Download contents of an existing file from specified path.

Client applications must keep track of data they have already downloaded and
where their position in the file is, and issue subsequent requests, with
modified offset, to gather the entire file.

Request does not carry size of requested chunk, the size is specified by
application itself. Note that file handles will remain open for consecutive
requests (as long as an idle timeout has not been reached and another
transport does not make use of uploading/downloading files using fs_mgmt),
but files are not exclusively owned by SMP, for the time of download
session, and may change between requests or even be removed.

classes.name: smp.file_management.FileDownloadResponse

class_variables.name: smp.file_management.FileDownloadResponse.off
documentation: Offset that this response is for.

class_variables.name: smp.file_management.FileDownloadResponse.data
documentation: Data read from the file.

class_variables.name: smp.file_management.FileDownloadResponse.len
documentation: The length of the file, only mandatory if “off” is 0.
Success response to a file download request.

classes.name: smp.file_management.FileUploadRequest

class_variables.name: smp.file_management.FileUploadRequest.off
documentation: Offset to start/continue writing to.

class_variables.name: smp.file_management.FileUploadRequest.data
documentation: Data to write to the file.

class_variables.name: smp.file_management.FileUploadRequest.name
documentation: Name of the file to upload.

class_variables.name: smp.file_management.FileUploadRequest.len
documentation: The length of the file, only mandatory if “off” is 0.

Upload a file to a specified location.

Command will automatically overwrite existing file or create a new one if it
does not exist at specified path. The protocol supports stateless upload
where each requests carries different chunk of a file and it is client side
responsibility to track progress of upload.

Note that file handles will remain open for consecutive requests (as long as
an idle timeout has not been reached, but files are not exclusively owned by
SMP, for the time of download session, and may change between requests or
even be removed. Note that file handles will remain open for consecutive
requests (as long as an idle timeout has not been reached and another
transport does not make use of uploading/downloading files using fs_mgmt),
but files are not exclusively owned by MCUmgr, for the time of download
session, and may change between requests or even be removed.

classes.name: smp.file_management.FileUploadResponse

class_variables.name: smp.file_management.FileUploadResponse.off
documentation: Offset of the file.
Success response to a file upload request.

classes.name: smp.file_management.FileStatusRequest
documentation: Retrieve status of an existing file from specified path of a target device.

classes.name: smp.file_management.FileStatusResponse
documentation: Success response to a file status request.

classes.name: smp.file_management.FileHashChecksumRequest

class_variables.name: smp.file_management.FileHashChecksumRequest.name
documentation: Absolute path to the file to generate hash/checksum for.

class_variables.name: smp.file_management.FileHashChecksumRequest.type
documentation: Type of hash/checksum to generate, if not provided, default is used.

class_variables.name: smp.file_management.FileHashChecksumRequest.off
documentation: Offset to start hash/checksum generation from, default 0.

class_variables.name: smp.file_management.FileHashChecksumRequest.len
documentation: Maximum length of the file to generate hash/checksum for, default is entire file.

Generate a hash/checksum of an existing file at a specified path on a target device.

Note that kernel heap memory is required for buffers to be allocated for
this to function, and large stack memory buffers are required for generation
of the output hash/checksum. Requires `CONFIG_MCUMGR_GRP_FS_CHECKSUM_HASH` to
be enabled for the base functionality, supported hash/checksum are opt-in
with `CONFIG_MCUMGR_GRP_FS_CHECKSUM_IEEE_CRC32` or
`CONFIG_MCUMGR_GRP_FS_HASH_SHA256`.

classes.name: smp.file_management.FileHashChecksumResponse

class_variables.name: smp.file_management.FileHashChecksumResponse.off
documentation: Only present if not 0.

class_variables.name: smp.file_management.FileHashChecksumResponse.len
documentation: Length of input data used to generate hash/checksum.

class_variables.name: smp.file_management.FileHashChecksumResponse.output
documentation: Output hash/checksum, depending on the type requested.
Success response to a file hash/checksum request.

classes.name: smp.file_management.SupportedFileHashChecksumTypesRequest
documentation:
List the hash and checksum types are available on a device.

Requires Kconfig `CONFIG_MCUMGR_GRP_FS_CHECKSUM_HASH_SUPPORTED_CMD` to be enabled.

classes.name: smp.file_management.HashChecksumFormat
documentation: Format that the hash/checksum returns where 0 is for numerical and 1 is for byte array.

classes.name: smp.file_management.HashChecksumType

class_variables.name: smp.file_management.HashChecksumType.format
documentation: Format that the hash/checksum returns where 0 is for numerical and 1 is for byte array.

class_variables.name: smp.file_management.HashChecksumType.size
documentation: Size of the hash/checksum in bytes.
Hash and checksum type supported by the device.

classes.name: smp.file_management.SupportedFileHashChecksumTypesResponse

class_variables.name: smp.file_management.SupportedFileHashChecksumTypesResponse.types
documentation: The map of supported hash/checksum types.
Success response to a supported file hash/checksum types request.

classes.name: smp.file_management.FileCloseRequest
documentation: Close all open file handles held by fs_mgmt upload/download requests.

classes.name: smp.file_management.FileCloseResponse
documentation: Success response to a file close request.

classes.name: smp.file_management.FS_MGMT_ERR

class_variables.name: smp.file_management.FS_MGMT_ERR.OK
documentation: No error (success).

class_variables.name: smp.file_management.FS_MGMT_ERR.UNKNOWN
documentation: Unknown error.

class_variables.name: smp.file_management.FS_MGMT_ERR.FILE_INVALID_NAME
documentation: The specified file name is not valid.

class_variables.name: smp.file_management.FS_MGMT_ERR.FILE_NOT_FOUND
documentation: The specified file does not exist.

class_variables.name: smp.file_management.FS_MGMT_ERR.FILE_IS_DIRECTORY
documentation: The specified file is a directory, not a file.

class_variables.name: smp.file_management.FS_MGMT_ERR.FILE_OPEN_FAILED
documentation: Error occurred whilst attempting to open a file.

class_variables.name: smp.file_management.FS_MGMT_ERR.FILE_SEEK_FAILED
documentation: Error occurred whilst attempting to seek to an offset in a file.

class_variables.name: smp.file_management.FS_MGMT_ERR.FILE_READ_FAILED
documentation: Error occurred whilst attempting to read data from a file.

class_variables.name: smp.file_management.FS_MGMT_ERR.FILE_TRUNCATE_FAILED
documentation: Error occurred whilst trying to truncate file.

class_variables.name: smp.file_management.FS_MGMT_ERR.FILE_DELETE_FAILED
documentation: Error occurred whilst trying to delete file.

class_variables.name: smp.file_management.FS_MGMT_ERR.FILE_WRITE_FAILED
documentation: Error occurred whilst attempting to write data to a file.

class_variables.name: smp.file_management.FS_MGMT_ERR.FILE_OFFSET_NOT_VALID
documentation:
The specified data offset is not valid, this could indicate that the file on the device has
changed since the previous command. The length of the current file on the device is returned as
"len", the user application needs to decide how to handle this (e.g. the hash of the file could
be requested and compared with the hash of the length of the file being uploaded to see if they
match or not).

class_variables.name: smp.file_management.FS_MGMT_ERR.FILE_OFFSET_LARGER_THAN_FILE
documentation: The requested offset is larger than the size of the file on the device.

class_variables.name: smp.file_management.FS_MGMT_ERR.CHECKSUM_HASH_NOT_FOUND
documentation: The requested checksum or hash type was not found or is not supported by this build.

class_variables.name: smp.file_management.FS_MGMT_ERR.MOUNT_POINT_NOT_FOUND
documentation: The specified mount point was not found or is not mounted.

class_variables.name: smp.file_management.FS_MGMT_ERR.READ_ONLY_FILESYSTEM
documentation: The specified mount point is that of a read-only filesystem.

class_variables.name: smp.file_management.FS_MGMT_ERR.FILE_EMPTY
documentation: The operation cannot be performed because the file is empty with no contents.
File System Management error codes.

documentation: File System Management error response.

classes.name: smp.file_management.FileSystemManagementErrorV2
documentation: File System Management error response.

modules.name: smp
documentation:
The Simple Management Protocol (SMP) for remotely managing MCU firmware.

This package implements de/serialization of SMP messages allowing for their use
on the transport of your choice.

The SMP specification can be found [here](https://docs.zephyrproject.org/latest/services/device_mgmt/smp_protocol.html).

## Usage

SMP messages are represented as Pydantic models.  Each SMP Request and
Response contains a `header` attribute that is an instance of
`smp.header.Header`.  Other attributes are specific to the message type.

For example, to create a `smp.os_management.EchoWriteRequest`:

```python
from smp.os_management import EchoWriteRequest

request = EchoWriteRequest(d="Hello world!")
print(bytes(request))
```

Prints the serialized SMP Frame:
```python
b'\n\x00\x00\x10\x00\x00\x00\x00\xa1adlHello world!'
```

All messages can be deserialized and validated using the `loads()` method.  To 
load a `smp.os_management.EchoWriteResponse`:

```python
from smp.os_management import EchoWriteResponse

data = bytes.fromhex("0b00000700008c00a1617263486921")  # data from the transport
response = EchoWriteResponse.loads(data)
print(response)
```

Prints the deserialized SMP message representation:
```
header=Header(op=<OP.WRITE_RSP: 3>, version=<Version.V2: 1>, flags=<Flag: 0>,
length=7, group_id=0, sequence=140, command_id=0) version=<Version.V2: 1>
sequence=140 smp_data=b'\x0b\x00\x00\x07\x00\x00\x8c\x00\xa1arcHi!' r='Hi!'
```
Generally, the `header` can be ignored and the message-specific attributes are
what you are interested in.
```
# print(response.r)
Hi!
```
All models and their attributes are statically typed and validated; enforced by
mypy linting and by Pydantic at runtime.

## Serialization

There are a few optional arguments that are common to all SMP messages when they
are created.

-   `header` is the SMP header.  Typically this can be left as `None` and will be
    so that the header is created automatically.
-   `version` is the SMP version.  This defaults to `smp.header.Version.V2`.
-   `sequence` is the sequence number of the message.  If not provided, it will
    be automatically generated using an incrementing counter.

Take a look at `smp.message` for more information on the base classes.

## Deserialization

If you are writing an SMP client, then you already know the type of the
incoming message because it must be a Response to your Request, or an
`smp.error.ErrorV1` or `smp.error.ErrorV2`.  You can use the
`smp.message._MessageBase.loads()` method that is common to all SMP
messages to deserialize and validate the message.

If you are writing an SMP server, then Python and SMP are odd choices!  Yet, you
can narrow the type by first loading the header with `smp.header.Header.loads()`.

## Encoding & Decoding

The USB and serial transports defined by Zephyr use a base64 encoding and
framing.  The encoding/fragmentation and decoding/reassembly is provided by 
`smp.packet.encode()` and `smp.packet.decode()`.

More information is at the [Zephyr docs](https://docs.zephyrproject.org/latest/services/device_mgmt/smp_transport.html#uart-serial-and-console)

## Typing

This package is meticulously typed and is intended to be used with mypy.

## Validation

All models are validated in order to detect transport and SMP server errors.  It
is impossible to create an invalid SMP message or deserialize an invalid SMP
message.  If you find a way, please open an issue.
flake8: noqa

modules.name: smp.settings_management
documentation: The Simple Management Protocol (SMP) Settings Management group.

classes.name: smp.settings_management.ReadSettingRequest

class_variables.name: smp.settings_management.ReadSettingRequest.name
documentation: The name of the setting to read.

class_variables.name: smp.settings_management.ReadSettingRequest.max_size
documentation: The maximum size of the data to read.
Read setting.

classes.name: smp.settings_management.ReadSettingResponse

class_variables.name: smp.settings_management.ReadSettingResponse.val
documentation:
Binary string of the returned data.

Note that the underlying data type cannot be specified through this and must
be known by the client.

class_variables.name: smp.settings_management.ReadSettingResponse.max_size
documentation:
The SMP server supports a smaller size than requested.

Will be set if the maximum supported data size is smaller than the maximum
requested data size, and contains the maximum data size which the device
supports, equivalent to `CONFIG_MCUMGR_GRP_SETTINGS_NAME_LEN`.
Read setting success response.

classes.name: smp.settings_management.WriteSettingRequest

class_variables.name: smp.settings_management.WriteSettingRequest.name
documentation: The name of the setting to write.

class_variables.name: smp.settings_management.WriteSettingRequest.val
documentation: Binary data to write.
Write setting.

classes.name: smp.settings_management.WriteSettingResponse
documentation: Write setting success response.

classes.name: smp.settings_management.DeleteSettingRequest

class_variables.name: smp.settings_management.DeleteSettingRequest.name
documentation: The name of the setting to delete.
Delete setting.

classes.name: smp.settings_management.DeleteSettingResponse
documentation: Delete setting success response.

classes.name: smp.settings_management.CommitSettingsRequest
documentation:
Commit pending settings.

Commit settings command allows committing all settings that have been set
but not yet applied on a device.

classes.name: smp.settings_management.CommitSettingsResponse
documentation: Commit pending settings success response.

classes.name: smp.settings_management.LoadSettingsRequest
documentation: Load settings from persistent storage.

classes.name: smp.settings_management.LoadSettingsResponse
documentation: Load settings from persistent storage success response.

classes.name: smp.settings_management.SaveSettingsRequest
documentation: Save settings to persistent storage.

classes.name: smp.settings_management.SaveSettingsResponse
documentation: Save settings to persistent storage success response.

documentation: Unknown error occurred.

class_variables.name: smp.settings_management.SETTINGS_MGMT_ERR.KEY_TOO_LONG
documentation: The provided key name is too long to be used.

class_variables.name: smp.settings_management.SETTINGS_MGMT_ERR.KEY_NOT_FOUND
documentation: The provided key name does not exist.

class_variables.name: smp.settings_management.SETTINGS_MGMT_ERR.READ_NOT_SUPPORTED
documentation: The provided key name does not support being read.

class_variables.name: smp.settings_management.SETTINGS_MGMT_ERR.ROOT_KEY_NOT_FOUND
documentation: The provided root key name does not exist.

class_variables.name: smp.settings_management.SETTINGS_MGMT_ERR.WRITE_NOT_SUPPORTED

class_variables.name: smp.settings_management.SETTINGS_MGMT_ERR.DELETE_NOT_SUPPORTED
documentation: The provided key name does not support being deleted.

classes.name: smp.settings_management.SettingsManagementErrorV2
documentation: Error response to a settings management command.

documentation: No error (success).

class_variables.name: smp.error.MGMT_ERR.EUNKNOWN
documentation: Unknown error.

class_variables.name: smp.error.MGMT_ERR.ENOMEM
documentation: Insufficient memory (likely not enough space for CBOR object).

class_variables.name: smp.error.MGMT_ERR.EINVAL
documentation: Error in input value.

class_variables.name: smp.error.MGMT_ERR.ETIMEOUT
documentation: Operation timed out.

class_variables.name: smp.error.MGMT_ERR.ENOENT
documentation: No such file/entry.

class_variables.name: smp.error.MGMT_ERR.EBADSTATE
documentation: Current state disallows command.

class_variables.name: smp.error.MGMT_ERR.EMSGSIZE
documentation: Response too large.

class_variables.name: smp.error.MGMT_ERR.ENOTSUP
documentation: Command not supported.

class_variables.name: smp.error.MGMT_ERR.ECORRUPT
documentation: Corrupt.

class_variables.name: smp.error.MGMT_ERR.EBUSY
documentation: Command blocked by processing of other command.

class_variables.name: smp.error.MGMT_ERR.EACCESSDENIED
documentation: Access to specific function, command or resource denied.

class_variables.name: smp.error.MGMT_ERR.UNSUPPORTED_TOO_OLD
documentation: Requested SMP MCUmgr protocol version is not supported (too old).

class_variables.name: smp.error.MGMT_ERR.UNSUPPORTED_TOO_NEW
documentation: Requested SMP MCUmgr protocol version is not supported (too new).

class_variables.name: smp.error.MGMT_ERR.EPERUSER

General error codes for the Simple Management Protocol (SMP).

class_variables.name: smp.enumeration_management.GroupDetailsRequest.groups
documentation:
Contains a list of the SMP group IDs to fetch details on.

If omitted, details on all supported groups will be returned.

Request the details of the supported SMP groups.

Details on supported groups command allows fetching details on each
supported SMP group, such as the name and number of handlers. A device can
specify an allow list of groups to return details on or details on all
groups can be returned.

This command is optional, it can be enabled using
`CONFIG_MCUMGR_GRP_ENUM_DETAILS`. The optional name and number of handlers
can be enabled/disabled with `CONFIG_MCUMGR_GRP_ENUM_DETAILS_NAME` and
`CONFIG_MCUMGR_GRP_ENUM_DETAILS_HANDLERS`.

modules.name: smp.packet
documentation: Generators for encoding and decoding Simple Management Protocol (SMP) packets.

functions.name: smp.packet.encode
documentation:
Iteratively pack an SMP bytes to packets of `line_length` size.

Note: only the USB/serial transport uses this encoding and fragmentation.
The BLE and UDP transports can simply send the SMP frames as they are.

Params:
- request: The bytes to be encoded.
- line_length: The maximum length of each packet.

Example:

Encode arbitrary bytes to SMP packets:

```python
from smp.packet import encode

for packet in encode(b"Hello, world!"):
    print(packet)
```
prints:
```
b'\x06\tAA9IZWxsbywgd29ybGQhet4=\n'
```

Set a smaller packet size to fragment:

```python
from smp.packet import encode

for packet in encode(b"Hello, world!", line_length=8):
    print(packet)
```
prints:
```
b'\x06\tAA9I\n'
b'\x04\x14ZWxs\n'
b'\x04\x14bywg\n'
b'\x04\x14d29y\n'
b'\x04\x14bGQh\n'
b'\x04\x14et4=\n'
```

The packet size should be set according to the buffer sizes of the SMP
server, and larger buffers will be more efficient.  It is not necessary to
use multiple buffers.

functions.name: smp.packet.decode
documentation:
Iteratively unpack a series of SMP packets to SMP frame bytes.

Example:

```python
from smp.packet import decode
from smp.image_management import ImageUploadWriteRequest

# Example encoded SMP packets
packets = (
    bytes.fromhex("06094148344b41414230414147610a"),
    bytes.fromhex("041441615a6a6247567544574e760a"),
    bytes.fromhex("04145a6d59594b6d4e7a6147464d0a"),
    bytes.fromhex("041464484a3163335167625755670a"),
    bytes.fromhex("0414596e4a765a475268644746590a"),
    bytes.fromhex("04145141414241674d45425159480a"),
    bytes.fromhex("041443416b4b4377774e446738510a"),
    bytes.fromhex("041445524954464255574678675a0a"),
    bytes.fromhex("04144768736348523466494345690a"),
    bytes.fromhex("04144979516c4a69636f4b536f720a"),
    bytes.fromhex("04144c4330754c7a41784d6a4d300a"),
    bytes.fromhex("04144e5459334f446b364f7a77390a"),
    bytes.fromhex("0414506a396c615731685a3255410a"),
    bytes.fromhex("04145a3356775a334a685a4758300a"),
    bytes.fromhex("04147134673d0a"),
)

decoder = decode()
next(decoder)

for packet in packets:
    try:
        decoder.send(packet)
    except StopIteration as e:
        frame = e.value
        break

print(frame)
print(ImageUploadWriteRequest.loads(frame))
```
prints:
```
bytearray(b'\n\x00\x00t\x00\x01\x9a\x01\xa6clen\rcoff\x18*cshaLtrust me broddataX@\x00\x01\x02\x03\x04\x05\x06\x07\x08\t\n\x0b\x0c\r\x0e\x0f\x10\x11\x12\x13\x14\x15\x16\x17\x18\x19\x1a\x1b\x1c\x1d\x1e\x1f !"#$%&\'()*+,-./0123456789:;<=>?eimage\x00gupgrade\xf4')  # noqa: E501
```
and
```
header=Header(op=<OP.WRITE: 2>, version=<Version.V2: 1>, flags=<Flag: 0>,
length=116, group_id=1, sequence=154, command_id=1) version=<Version.V2: 1>
sequence=154 smp_data=b'\n\x00\x00t\x00\x01\x9a\x01\xa6clen\rcoff\x18*cshaL
trust me broddataX@\x00\x01\x02\x03\x04\x05\x06\x07\x08\t\n\x0b\x0c\r\x0e
\x0f\x10\x11\x12\x13\x14\x15\x16\x17\x18\x19\x1a\x1b\x1c\x1d\x1e\x1f !"#$%&
\'()*+,-./0123456789:;<=>?eimage\x00gupgrade\xf4' off=42 data=b'\x00\x01\x02
\x03\x04\x05\x06\x07\x08\t\n\x0b\x0c\r\x0e\x0f\x10\x11\x12\x13\x14\x15\x16
\x17\x18\x19\x1a\x1b\x1c\x1d\x1e\x1f !"#$%&\'()*+,-./0123456789:;<=>?'
image=0 len=13 sha=b'trust me bro' upgrade=False
```

modules.name: smp.image_management
documentation:
The Simple Management Protocol (SMP) Image Management group.

## Notion of "slots" and "images" in Zephyr

The “slot” and “image” definition comes from mcuboot where “image” would consist
of two “slots”, further named “primary” and “secondary”; the application is
supposed to run from the “primary slot” and update is supposed to be uploaded to
the “secondary slot”; the mcuboot is responsible in swapping slots on boot. This
means that pair of slots is dedicated to single upgradable application. In case
of Zephyr this gets a little bit confusing because DTS will use
“slot0_partition” and “slot1_partition”, as label of fixed-partition dedicated
to single application, but will name them as “image-0” and “image-1”
respectively.

Currently Zephyr supports at most two images, in which case mapping is as
follows:

| Image | Slot labels | Slot Names |
|-------|-------------|------------|
| 0     | “slot0_partition” “slot1_partition” | “image-0” “image-1” |
| 1     | “slot2_partition” “slot3_partition” | “image-2” “image-3” |

classes.name: smp.image_management.HashBytes
documentation: Only to print something useful to the `rich` console.

classes.name: smp.image_management.ImageState

class_variables.name: smp.image_management.ImageState.slot
documentation:
Slot number within “image”.

Each image has two slots:
- primary (running one) = 0
- secondary (for DFU dual-bank purposes) = 1.

class_variables.name: smp.image_management.ImageState.version
documentation: Version of the image.

class_variables.name: smp.image_management.ImageState.image
documentation:
Semi-optional image number.

The field is not required when only one image is supported by the running
application.

class_variables.name: smp.image_management.ImageState.hash
documentation:
SHA256 hash of the image header and body.

Note that this will not be the same as the SHA256 of the whole file, it is
the field in the MCUboot TLV section that contains a hash of the data which
is used for signature verification purposes. This field is optional but only
optional when using MCUboot's serial recovery feature with one pair of image
slots, `CONFIG_BOOT_SERIAL_IMG_GRP_HASH` can be disabled to remove
support for hashes in this configuration. SMP server in applications must
support sending hashes.

class_variables.name: smp.image_management.ImageState.bootable
documentation:
True if image has bootable flag set.

This field does not have to be present if false.

class_variables.name: smp.image_management.ImageState.pending
documentation:
True if image is set for next swap.

This field does not have to be present if false.

class_variables.name: smp.image_management.ImageState.confirmed
documentation:
True if image has been confirmed.

This field does not have to be present if false.

class_variables.name: smp.image_management.ImageState.active
documentation:
True if image is currently active application

This field does not have to be present if false.

class_variables.name: smp.image_management.ImageState.permanent
documentation:
True if image is to stay in primary slot after the next boot.

This does not have to be present if false.
The state of an image in a slot.

classes.name: smp.image_management.ImageStatesReadRequest
documentation: Obtain list of images with their current state.

classes.name: smp.image_management.ImageStatesReadResponse

class_variables.name: smp.image_management.ImageStatesReadResponse.images
documentation: List of images with their current state.

class_variables.name: smp.image_management.ImageStatesReadResponse.splitStatus
documentation:
States whether loader of split image is compatible with application part.

This is unused by Zephyr.
Response to an image state request.

classes.name: smp.image_management.ImageStatesWriteRequest

class_variables.name: smp.image_management.ImageStatesWriteRequest.hash
documentation: SHA256 hash of the image header and body.

class_variables.name: smp.image_management.ImageStatesWriteRequest.confirm
documentation:
Confirm the image given by hash.

CAUTION: it is dangerous to confirm the image before it has been booted!

Setting this to true will mark the image as confirmed before it has been
booted, which can brick the device.  Zephyr provides hooks for confirming
the image from within the application, so this is not necessary for normal
operation.

Set the state of an image.

If “confirm” is false or not provided, an image with the “hash” will be set
for test, which means that it will not be marked as permanent and upon hard
reset the previous application will be restored to the primary slot. In case
when “confirm” is true, the “hash” is optional as the currently running
application will be assumed as target for confirmation.

classes.name: smp.image_management.ImageStatesWriteResponse
documentation: Success response to an image state write request.

classes.name: smp.image_management.ImageUploadWriteRequest

class_variables.name: smp.image_management.ImageUploadWriteRequest.off
documentation: The offset of the data chunk in this request.

class_variables.name: smp.image_management.ImageUploadWriteRequest.data
documentation: The data chunk to write.

class_variables.name: smp.image_management.ImageUploadWriteRequest.image
documentation:
Image number.

It does not have to appear in request at all, in which case it is assumed to
be 0. Should only be present when `off` is 0.

class_variables.name: smp.image_management.ImageUploadWriteRequest.len
documentation:
The length of the image.

Required when `off` is 0, otherwise ignored.

class_variables.name: smp.image_management.ImageUploadWriteRequest.sha
documentation:
SHA256 hash of the image.

This is used to identify an upload session (e.g. to allow to continue
a broken session), and for image verification purposes. This must be a full
SHA256 hash of the whole image being uploaded, or not included if the hash
is not available (in which case, upload session continuation and image
verification functionality will be unavailable). Should only be present when
“off” is 0.

class_variables.name: smp.image_management.ImageUploadWriteRequest.upgrade
documentation:
Optional flag that states that only upgrade should be allowed.

If the version of uploaded software is not higher then already on a device,
the image upload will be rejected. Zephyr compares major, minor and
revision (x.y.z) by default unless `CONFIG_MCUMGR_GRP_IMG_VERSION_CMP_USE_BUILD_NUMBER`
is set, whereby it will compare build numbers too. Should only be present
when “off” is 0.

Upload an image to the device.

The image is uploaded in chunks, with each chunk being sent in a separate
request. The first request must include the image's length and image number.

classes.name: smp.image_management.ImageUploadWriteResponse

class_variables.name: smp.image_management.ImageUploadWriteResponse.off
documentation:
The portion of the upload that has been completed, in 8-bit bytes.

This is the offset of the next byte to be written. If the offset is equal to
the length of the image, the upload is complete.

class_variables.name: smp.image_management.ImageUploadWriteResponse.match
documentation:
Indicates if the uploaded data successfully matches the provided SHA256.

Only sent in the final packet if CONFIG_IMG_ENABLE_IMAGE_CHECK is enabled.

class_variables.name: smp.image_management.ImageUploadWriteResponse.rc
documentation:
Legacy field that contains a return code; possibly `MGMT_ERR`.

This field may be present on old SMP server implementations or new SMP
server implementations that have set
`CONFIG_MCUMGR_SMP_LEGACY_RC_BEHAVIOUR=y` for backwards compatibility with
old SMP clients.

Note that we are not validating this field because we don't necessarily
trust the server to send us valid values. If this value is present, then it
indicates use of an SMP server that is out of spec and interpretation of the
value should be done with reference to that server's source code, rather
that the SMP specification.

Zephyr source code reference: https://github.com/zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr/blob/91a1e706535b2f99433280513c5bc66dfb918506/subsys/mgmt/mcumgr/grp/img_mgmt/src/img_mgmt.c#L397-L400
Success response to an image upload request.

class_variables.name: smp.image_management.IMG_MGMT_ERR.FLASH_READ_FAILED

class_variables.name: smp.image_management.IMG_MGMT_ERR.FLASH_WRITE_FAILED

class_variables.name: smp.image_management.IMG_MGMT_ERR.FLASH_ERASE_FAILED

class_variables.name: smp.image_management.IMG_MGMT_ERR.INVALID_SLOT

class_variables.name: smp.image_management.IMG_MGMT_ERR.NO_FREE_MEMORY

class_variables.name: smp.image_management.IMG_MGMT_ERR.FLASH_CONTEXT_ALREADY_SET

class_variables.name: smp.image_management.IMG_MGMT_ERR.FLASH_CONTEXT_NOT_SET

class_variables.name: smp.image_management.IMG_MGMT_ERR.FLASH_AREA_DEVICE_NULL
documentation: The device for the flash area is NULL.

class_variables.name: smp.image_management.IMG_MGMT_ERR.INVALID_PAGE_OFFSET
documentation: The offset for a page number is invalid.

class_variables.name: smp.image_management.IMG_MGMT_ERR.INVALID_OFFSET
documentation: The offset parameter was not provided and is required.

class_variables.name: smp.image_management.IMG_MGMT_ERR.INVALID_LENGTH
documentation: The length parameter was not provided and is required.

class_variables.name: smp.image_management.IMG_MGMT_ERR.INVALID_IMAGE_HEADER
documentation: The image length is smaller than the size of an image header.

class_variables.name: smp.image_management.IMG_MGMT_ERR.INVALID_IMAGE_HEADER_MAGIC

class_variables.name: smp.image_management.IMG_MGMT_ERR.INVALID_HASH

class_variables.name: smp.image_management.IMG_MGMT_ERR.INVALID_FLASH_ADDRESS

class_variables.name: smp.image_management.IMG_MGMT_ERR.VERSION_GET_FAILED

class_variables.name: smp.image_management.IMG_MGMT_ERR.CURRENT_VERSION_IS_NEWER

class_variables.name: smp.image_management.IMG_MGMT_ERR.IMAGE_ALREADY_PENDING

class_variables.name: smp.image_management.IMG_MGMT_ERR.INVALID_IMAGE_VECTOR_TABLE

class_variables.name: smp.image_management.IMG_MGMT_ERR.INVALID_IMAGE_TOO_LARGE

class_variables.name: smp.image_management.IMG_MGMT_ERR.IMAGE_CONFIRMATION_DENIED

class_variables.name: smp.image_management.IMG_MGMT_ERR.IMAGE_SETTING_TEST_TO_ACTIVE_DENIED
documentation: Setting test to active slot is not allowed

classes.name: smp.os_management.ResetWriteRequest

class_variables.name: smp.os_management.ResetWriteRequest.force
documentation:
Force reset.

Normally the command sends an empty CBOR map as data, but if a previous
reset attempt has responded with “rc” equal to MGMT_ERR_EBUSY then the
following map may be sent to force a reset

Performs reset of system.

The device should issue response before resetting so that the SMP client
could receive information that the command has been accepted. By default,
this command is accepted in all conditions, however if the
`CONFIG_MCUMGR_GRP_OS_RESET_HOOK` is enabled and an application registers a
callback, the callback will be called when this command is issued and can be
used to perform any necessary tidy operations prior to the module rebooting,
or to reject the reset request outright altogether with an error response.

For details on this functionality, see [callbacks](https://docs.zephyrproject.org/latest/services/device_mgmt/mcumgr_callbacks.html#mcumgr-callbacks).  # noqa: E501

class_variables.name: smp.os_management.TaskStatistics.stkuse
documentation:
Stack usage.

The unit is system dependent and in case of Zephyr this is number of 4 byte words.

documentation:
Stack size.

The unit is system dependent and in case of Zephyr this is number of 4 byte words.

classes.name: smp.os_management.OSApplicationInfoReadRequest

class_variables.name: smp.os_management.OSApplicationInfoReadRequest.format
documentation:
Format specifier of returned response.

Fields are appended in their natural ascending index order, not the order
of characters that are received by the command.

Format specifiers:
* `s` Kernel name
* `n` Node name
* `r` Kernel release
* `v` Kernel version
* `b` Build date and time (requires `CONFIG_MCUMGR_GRP_OS_INFO_BUILD_DATE_TIME`)
* `m` Machine
* `p` Processor
* `i` Hardware platform
* `o` Operating system
* `a` All fields (shorthand for all above options)

If this option is not provided, the `s` Kernel name option will be used.
Request information about the application running on the device.

classes.name: smp.os_management.OSApplicationInfoReadResponse

class_variables.name: smp.os_management.OSApplicationInfoReadResponse.output
documentation: Text response including requested parameters.
Success response to an application information request.

classes.name: smp.os_management.BootloaderInformationReadRequest

class_variables.name: smp.os_management.BootloaderInformationReadRequest.query
documentation:
Is string representing query for parameters.

With no restrictions how the query looks like as processing of query is left
for bootloader backend. If there is no query, then response will return string
identifying the bootloader.

MCUboot supports the query string,"mode".  The response to mode is of type
`MCUbootMode`.
Request bootloader information.

classes.name: smp.os_management.MCUbootModeQueryResponse
documentation: Response to a MCUboot mode query.

classes.name: smp.os_management.BootloaderInformationReadResponse

class_variables.name: smp.os_management.BootloaderInformationReadResponse.bootloader
documentation: String identifying the bootloader.  For MCUboot it will be "MCUboot".

class_variables.name: smp.os_management.BootloaderInformationReadResponse.response
documentation:
Response to “query”.

This is optional and may be left out in case when query yields no response,
SMP version 2 error code of `OS_MGMT_ERR_QUERY_YIELDS_NO_ANSWER` is
expected. Response may have more than one parameter reported back or it may
be a map, that is dependent on bootloader backend and query.
Success response to a bootloader information request.

classes.name: smp.os_management.OS_MGMT_RET_RC

class_variables.name: smp.os_management.OS_MGMT_RET_RC.OK
documentation: No error, this is implied if there is no ret value in the response.

class_variables.name: smp.os_management.OS_MGMT_RET_RC.UNKNOWN
documentation: Unknown error occurred.

class_variables.name: smp.os_management.OS_MGMT_RET_RC.INVALID_FORMAT
documentation: The provided format value is not valid.

class_variables.name: smp.os_management.OS_MGMT_RET_RC.QUERY_YIELDS_NO_ANSWER
documentation: Query was not recognized.

class_variables.name: smp.os_management.OS_MGMT_RET_RC.RTC_NOT_SET
documentation: RTC is not set.

class_variables.name: smp.os_management.OS_MGMT_RET_RC.RTC_COMMAND_FAILED
documentation: RTC command failed.
